user_outcome: Running `sworn loop --release <name>` drives that release's autonomous loop without also passing `--parallel` — board-driven mode is the default whenever `--release` is given, and the dead `--base` flag is removed from the flag surface entirely.
rationale: cmd/sworn/run.go currently gates board-driven mode on `if *parallel` alone (line 118); `--release` without `--parallel` falls through to the `--task` ad-hoc bootstrap path and errors. `--parallel`'s name never matched its purpose (it gates "read from a release board," not "run tracks concurrently" — even a one-track release needs it today). Separately, `--base` (line 38) is parsed via `fs.String("base", "main", ...)` then immediately discarded (`_ = base`) and never dereferenced again in the file — dead code, folded into this slice per the planning decision since it's the same file and the same flag-parsing block. `--release <name>` becomes the permanent, concurrency-safe, standalone way to drive a specific release (multiple terminals can each pass a different `--release` with zero shared state) — it is NOT deprecated by S03's convenience layer.

effort_complexity.rationale: Flag-gating condition change (one boolean expression) + remove one dead flag + update the two guard checks (--resume, --dry-run) that currently key off *parallel alone. Confined to cmd/sworn/run.go.

acceptance_criteria.text: When `sworn loop --release <name>` is run WITHOUT `--parallel`, it SHALL drive the board-driven (release) path exactly as `--release <name> --parallel` does today (identical behavior to the pre-change `--parallel --release <name>` invocation).

`sworn loop --release <name> --parallel` (both flags together) SHALL continue to work exactly as before this change — `--parallel` remains an accepted, harmless flag; this slice does not remove it or change its meaning, only makes it optional when `--release` is present.
If neither `--release` nor `--task` is given, `sworn loop` SHALL fail closed with a usage error exactly as before this change (this slice does not touch the zero-flag case — that is S03's scope).

The `--base` flag SHALL be removed from `cmd/sworn/run.go`'s flag set entirely (not just left discarded) — it was dead code (parsed at line 38, discarded via `_ = base`, never dereferenced elsewhere in the file) and served no function for either the `--task` or `--release` paths.

After the change, `go build ./...` SHALL succeed and `go test ./cmd/sworn/...` SHALL pass, including a new test asserting `sworn run --release <name>` (no `--parallel`) reaches the same code path as `--release <name> --parallel` (e.g. both produce the same "--release is required" / dispatch behavior for a given input, verified without a real model call per this package's existing table-driven-fake test convention).
